about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Green had a lot to do with it. Because Joe wrote the tune, 'Soothe Me,' and I supplied the bridge.

about_quotes.speaker: Don Menza and Sam Noto
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee narrates a story about Hank (Mancini) and a fortune teller who mentions the name 'Green' (Joe Green). The interviewee does not claim a personal relationship with Joe Green, only that they are aware of the anecdote.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Menza and Sam Noto interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Green would build a tune for us... The reeds would get into this side of the hall... And I'd check it off.

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: Then, there was a guy in Cleveland, a big boy, Joe Green, or something.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: When I worked later with Nat Shilkret and -- Green, Joe Green.
